titleStmt.title: SII 11.34: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 34. (B.K. No. 162 of 1926-27). ASUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB SET UP BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF UḌACHAVVA.

summary.p: The record refers itself to the reign of Nityavarsha and is dated in Śaka 847, Pārthiva (= A.D. 925). Since Gōvinda IV was the ruling king in that year, it has to be surmised that he also had the surname Nityavarsha like his father Indra III. (See Annual Report on Epigraphy 1916, Pt. ii, para. 38). The inscription registers a gift of land made by Nāgayya to a temple built by him while Chandraprabha-Bhaṭāra of the Dhōra-Jinālaya of Ba[ṅka]pura was administering Pasuṇḍi (i.e., modern Asuṇḍi) and Nāgapuli Gāvuṇḍa was the head-man (of the village.)

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
nityavarṣa śrīpṛthuvīvallaThe left
hārājadhirāja paramēśvara pa
ṭṭārakara śrīvijayarājya mu
ttarōttarābhivṛddhigaṁ saluttire sakanṛpa
kālātītasaṁvatsaraśataṅga ḷeṇṭunū
ṟenālvattēḻaneya prārtthiva saṁvatsa
raṁ pravarttisa baṅkapurada dhōra jinā
layada candraprabha bhaṭārā rppasu
ṇḍiya nabhyantarasiddhiyāḷuttire nāgapuli
gāvuṇḍaṁ gāvuṇḍugeyye rappura nāgayyaṁ
tanna māḍisida dēgulakkendu nāgapuli
gāvuṇḍana kayyoḻaṁ pannirmmattarkkeyyaṁ māṟu
goṇḍubiṭṭanā pannirmmattarkkeygaṁ nālkugeydu dēva
manyarggāgi bāḻvudu Alliṁ mēgādu dinyarenāḷvonu
gāvuṇḍugeyvātanu kāduḍuvudu Ī sthitiya naḻidōṁ
ge vāraṇāsiyumanī liṅgamumanī kavileyuma
naḻkōṭi tapōdhanaruma naḻida pāpa makku mida
kāduḍidōṁge vāraṇāsiyoḷsāsira kavileyaṁ
kōḍuṁ koḷagumaṁ ponnoḷkaṭṭisi dānaṁgoṭṭa pha
lamakku © baredōṁ lōkayyaṁ besageyda nā
singaṁ [.] maṅgaḷaṁ [.]
Digital edition of SII 11.34 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
